---
title: "Question 3 - Paired t-test (Regular vs Premium fuel mileage)"
author: "Upayan Mazumder"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
alpha <- 0.05
```

## Question

Use a paired t-test to check whether mileage on premium fuel is significantly better than regular fuel.

```{r data}
# Values read from the image table
regular <- c(16, 20, 21, 22, 23, 22, 27, 25, 27, 28)
premium <- c(19, 22, 24, 24, 25, 25, 26, 26, 28)

# The image has one fewer premium entry; use complete paired observations
n_pairs <- min(length(regular), length(premium))
paired_data <- data.frame(
  Car = 1:n_pairs,
  Regular = regular[1:n_pairs],
  Premium = premium[1:n_pairs]
)

paired_data
```

## Hypotheses

- H0: mean(Premium - Regular) <= 0
- H1: mean(Premium - Regular) > 0

```{r paired-t-test}
diff <- paired_data$Premium - paired_data$Regular
summary(diff)

tt <- t.test(
  paired_data$Premium,
  paired_data$Regular,
  paired = TRUE,
  alternative = "greater"
)

tt
```

```{r decision}
if (tt$p.value < alpha) {
  cat("Decision: Reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: Premium fuel gives significantly higher mileage than regular fuel.")
} else {
  cat("Decision: Fail to reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: There is not enough evidence that premium fuel gives better mileage.")
}
```
